items.title: AMD B650 expansion cards hit retail starting at $199 — add four M.2 PCIe 4.0 slots and 11 USB ports to any PC with a PCIe slot
items.description: A couple of new add-in cards exemplify the trend of slapping AMD's Promontory 21 chipset onto a card for extra I/O expansion.
items.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 1 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter It's a weird fact of the modern PC industry, but the motherboard "chipset" or "Platform Controller Hub" (to use Intel's terminology) is basically just a PCIe-attached I/O hub that exposes additional PCIe, USB, SATA, and platform services. Contemporary CPUs are built as SoCs, so all the essential functions to run the machine are integrated into the CPU itself. That's why companies have started creating what are effectively I/O breakout boards by slapping motherboard chipsets onto PCI Express cards, including the one pictured above from WisdPi, as well as one coming from Minisforum, as spotted by PC Watch at Computex.In the case of the AMD Promontory 21 chipset, it really is just a PCI Express I/O controller. In fact, AMD created the X670 chipset by simply wiring up two Promontory 21 chips in series. It's the exact same chip, just used in pairs on the fancier motherboards, so you end up with piles of I/O, though it's all running over the same PCI Express 4.0 x4 link to the CPU. Two of the M.2 slots are on the front of the card, while a large black heatsink covers the Promontory 21 chip. (Image credit: WisdPi)We've reported on cards like this before; this past December, we highlighted an open-source project for exactly this purpose, and of course, Asusd and ASRock have shipped expansion boards for specific motherboards that "upgrade" them from B650 to X670 by adding the second Promontory 21 chipset. All the way back in 2023, we also covered AMD engineers showing off the same thing in a Gamers Nexus video. Indeed, the very word "Promontory" refers to a projection from a larger body, such as a high cliff or, perhaps, an add-on card.Latest Videos FromWatch full video here: So, the WisdPi "PROM21 All In Expansion Card" is a half-height PCI Express 4.0 add-in card that offers a whopping four M.2 slots, five USB 10 Gbps ports, six USB 2.0 ports, and an OCuLink port that supports PCI Express 4.0 or can breakout into four SATA ports. That's more connectivity than some motherboards offer, which is no surprise, since the card costs $199 by itself, with no cables. WisdPi is primarily a Raspberry Pi shop, and as you'd expect, the card is compatible with Raspberry Pi devices that expose PCI Express, but that's simply because it's actually compatible with any PCI Express device, from AMD, Intel, Arm, or whoever. The other two M.2 slots are on the back of the card, which could complicate cooling. (Image credit: WisdPi)The card spotted by PC Watch at Minisforum's Computex booth is similar in that it also offers four M.2 slots, an OCuLink connector, and at least one high-speed 20 Gbps USB port. However, it lacks the SATA functionality and the other USB ports of the WisdPi offering. Also, unlike WisdPi's card, it seems that it will come with a dedicated cooling unit (with a blower fan and a shroud) for fast SSDs. That's a little ironic considering the card seemingly limits drives to PCIe 4.0 x2 if you install all four, but "cooling" is better than "no cooling". No word on how much the Minisforum offering will cost, but it will apparently be available in Q3 of this year. You may like This platypus PCIe adapter converts half-height GPUs into full-height while adding two M.2 slots for SSDs New flat M.2 SSD adapter doesn't stick out from the motherboard, unlocking PCIe slots hidden under massive GPUs ASRock launches new Frankensteined motherboard with one DDR4 slot and two DDR5 slots It's almost a shame these cards aren't more commonplace. For folks with machines based on entry-level motherboards, one of these cards could be just the ticket to expanding the I/O. Particularly on older-generation motherboards that usually had only one M.2 slot (if any), a card like one of these could enable significant storage expansion now that M.2 SSDs are the only type still being actively produced. You can check out PC Watch's photos of the Minisforum "PCIE TO 4" card at their post over here.Follow Tom's Hardware on Google News, or add us as a preferred source, to get our latest news, analysis, & reviews in your feeds.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsors
items.author: Zak Killian
items.titleZh: AMD B650扩展卡上市零售价199美元起——为任何拥有PCIe插槽的PC添加四个M.2 PCIe 4.0插槽和11个USB端口
items.descriptionZh:
现代PC行业存在一个奇特现象：主板“芯片组”或英特尔所谓的“平台控制器中枢”（Platform Controller Hub），本质上只是一个通过PCIe连接的I/O集线器，用于提供额外的PCIe、USB、SATA及平台服务。如今的CPU已集成SoC设计，运行机器所需的核心功能都包含在CPU内部。因此，一些公司开始将主板芯片组直接封装到PCI Express扩展卡上，做成实际的I/O扩展板——例如WisdPi推出的PROM21 All In扩展卡，以及Computex展会上Minisforum展示的类似产品（PC Watch报道）。

### 核心技术原理与创新点

以AMD Promontory 21芯片组为例，它本质上就是一个PCI Express I/O控制器。有趣的是，AMD在高端X670主板上通过串联两颗Promontory 21芯片来实现更多I/O接口——其实就是同一颗芯片，在高端主板上成对使用，从而获得大量I/O扩展能力，但所有这些接口都通过同一条PCI Express 4.0 x4链路连接到CPU。WisdPi的“PROM21 All In Expansion Card”正是基于这一设计，将Promontory 21芯片作为半高PCIe 4.0扩展卡推出。卡正面覆盖大型黑色散热片，两个M.2插槽设于正面，另两个M.2插槽位于背面（可能影响散热），此外还有五个USB 10Gbps端口、六个USB 2.0端口和一个支持PCIe 4.0的OCuLink端口（可扩展为四个SATA端口）。该卡本身售价199美元（不含线缆）。

此前类似产品已有报道：2023年12月我们报道过一个开源项目，华硕和华擎也推出过针对特定主板的扩展板，可从B650“升级”到X670（即添加第二颗Promontory 21芯片）。早在2023年，AMD工程师就在Gamers Nexus视频中展示过相同概念。有趣的是，“Promontory”一词本意是“从较大山体突出的部分（如悬崖）”，恰好对应这种附加卡的角色。

### 性能参数与对比

WisdPi卡提供四个M.2插槽（PCIe 4.0）、五个10Gbps USB口、六个USB 2.0、一个OCuLink（可转4个SATA）。这一连接能力甚至超过某些主板的原生接口，但注意所有I/O共享一条PCIe 4.0 x4链路（约8GB/s带宽）。该卡兼容任何PCIe设备（AMD、Intel、Arm平台），包括树莓派等单板计算机——只要它们有PCIe接口。

Minisforum在Computex上展示的类似卡（暂名“PCIE TO 4”）同样提供四个M.2插槽、一个OCuLink接口和至少一个20Gbps高速USB端口，但缺少SATA功能和其他USB端口。值得注意的是，Minisforum版似乎会配备专用散热单元（含吹风风扇和导流罩），适合高速SSD——这有点讽刺，因为如果全部安装四个M.2 SSD，每个仅能获得PCIe 4.0 x2带宽，但“有散热”总比“没散热”好。Minisforum卡预计2024年Q3上市，价格未公布。

### 技术影响与应用场景

这类扩展卡尤其适合使用入门级主板（通常只有一两个M.2插槽）的用户，特别是老一代主板往往只有一个甚至没有M.2插槽。在现代M.2 SSD是唯一仍在积极生产的存储类型的背景下，这类卡可大幅扩展存储能力。对于树莓派等小型PCIe设备，它们也能瞬间获得丰富的I/O扩展。可惜这些卡尚未普及——如果能更常见，将极大提升旧平台和小型系统的I/O灵活性。感兴趣的读者可以查看PC Watch关于Minisforum卡的详细照片报道。

items.title: Upgrade your PC with a 2TB PCIe 4.0 SSD, 750W power supply, and 240mm AIO for $300 — hardware bundle saves you nearly $200
items.description: Even though power supplies and CPU coolers have remained unaffected by the AI boom, storage costs have skyrocketed, which makes this deal even more lucrative. You're getting a blazing-fast 2TB SSD, a genuinely solid power supply, and a decent AIO that can help you stop worrying about the small things and focus on sourcing cheaper memory and GPUs.
items.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 0 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter Despite the AI boom driving up prices and making it harder to build a PC today, there are still some good deals floating around. We've just found one from MSI, and it's a bundle that includes a 2TB PCIe 4.0 SSD, a 240mm AIO liquid cooler, and a 750W 80+ Gold power supply with native ATX 3.1 compatibility. It's called the "SPATIUM M470 PRO 2TB SSD Refresh Kit," and it's available for just $299 right now, down from $492. Check out this deal on the MSI Store As the name suggests, the bundle includes an MSI Spatium M470 Pro SSD in a 2TB capacity. We've reviewed the non-Pro variant of this drive and found it adequate. This "Pro" model is actually DRAM-less but still features sequential read speeds up to 6,000 MB/s and write speeds up to 5,000 MB/s, with up to 640 TBW of endurance. The Phison E27T controller powers it and comes with a 5-year warranty.Next up, there's a MAG CoreLiquid A13 240mm AIO part of the kit, too. It's a pretty run-of-the-mill liquid cooler that supports recent CPU families. It has ARGB fans and subtle lighting on the pump block, with a pretty minimal overall design. The pump speed can be modulated between 300 RPM and 3,800 RPM, with the fans reaching up to 200 RPM, pushing up to 62.6 CFM of airflow. We found the CoreLiquid A13 to be a decent option in our review — nothing special, but it can definitely get the job done. Save 39% ($192.01)MSI Spatium M470 Pro 2TB SSD Refresh Kit: was $492 now $299.99 at AmazonThis bundle includes a Spatium M470 Pro 2TB SSD with PCIe 4.0 x4 connectivity for blazing fast speeds. You also get a MAG CoreLiquid A13 240mm AIO with ARGB fans and pump. Lastly, the MAG A750GLS power supply rounds out the kit with its 80+ Gold rating and native 16-pin connectors.View Deal Lastly, you'll also get the MAG A750GLS power supply in the bundle; it's a 750W PCIe 5.0 unit with an 80+ Gold rating. That means it comes with a native 16-pin connector to power the hungriest GPUs, and the cable has a yellow end that can help identify loose connections. A 10-year warranty backs the A750GLS and comes with enough sleeved cables to cover all your needs. It's also a well-reviewed model in the community.All in all, you're building a solid base with these components that can help make the hunt for more important parts, such as the GPU and memory, easier. For just $299 on the MSI Store right now, it doesn't get much better than this. Even if you were to go for a cheaper cooler and SSD on its own and ditch the bundle, it would cost you around $400 to source the parts individually, so don't miss out on this deal! If you're looking for more savings, check out our Best PC Hardware deals for a range of products, or dive deeper into our specialized SSD and Storage Deals, Hard Drive Deals, Gaming Monitor Deals, Graphics Card Deals, gaming chair, or CPU Deals pages.
items.author: Hassam Nasir
items.titleZh: 用2TB PCIe 4.0固态硬盘、750W电源和240mm一体式水冷升级你的电脑，仅需300美元——硬件套装省近200美元
items.descriptionZh:
在AI浪潮推动下，PC硬件价格普遍上涨、组装难度增加，但市场上仍存在一些高性价比的选择。微星（MSI）近期推出了一款名为“SPATIUM M470 PRO 2TB SSD Refresh Kit”的套装，包含一块2TB PCIe 4.0固态硬盘、一个240mm一体式水冷散热器，以及一个750W 80+ Gold认证、原生ATX 3.1兼容的电源，目前售价仅为299美元（原价492美元），降幅达39%。该套装在MSI官方商店有售。

套装核心之一是微星Spatium M470 Pro固态硬盘，容量2TB。这款“Pro”型号采用无DRAM缓存设计，但依然能提供高达6000 MB/s的顺序读取速度和5000 MB/s的顺序写入速度，耐久度达到640 TBW。它由群联Phison E27T主控驱动，并享有5年质保。虽然我们此前评测过非Pro版本且认为表现尚可，但Pro版本在无DRAM方案中属于性能较好的选择。

散热部分为MAG CoreLiquid A13 240mm一体式水冷，属于中规中矩的常规产品，支持主流CPU平台。它配备ARGB风扇，泵头带有柔和灯光，整体设计简约。水泵转速可在300-3800 RPM之间调节，风扇最高转速2000 RPM（原文可能笔误写为200 RPM？实际应为2000 RPM左右），最大风量62.6 CFM。在我们的评测中，该水冷表现不错，虽不惊艳但足以胜任日常散热需求。

电源是MAG A750GLS，额定功率750W，符合PCIe 5.0标准，获得80+ Gold金牌认证。它配备原生16针（12VHPWR）接口，可以为高端显卡供电，并且该线缆末端采用黄色设计以帮助用户识别是否插紧。电源提供10年质保，附带足够多的编制线缆，满足各类装机需求，在社区中口碑良好。

总体而言，这套装机核心部件为组装一台高性能PC奠定了扎实基础，后续只需再购买显卡和内存即可。如果单独购买类似规格的散热器、SSD和电源，总价大约需要400美元，而套装仅299美元，性价比非常突出。该套装适合正在攒机、追求高速存储和稳定供电的用户，尤其适合升级老平台或搭建中高配游戏主机。如需更多优惠信息，可关注PC硬件特价、SSD存储、硬盘、游戏显示器、显卡、游戏椅及CPU等专项折扣页面。

items.title: G.Skill explains how AMD EXPO ULL unlocks additional performance — expanded profiles allow memory makers to include subtiming tweaks for the first time
items.description: AMD's EXPO Ultra Low Latency program, announced at Computex 2026, aims to give users a one-click route to lower memory latencies than its existing EXPO profiles
items.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 1 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter AMD's EXPO Ultra Low Latency program, announced at Computex 2026, aims to give users a one-click route to lower memory latencies than its existing EXPO profiles, but the company's initial announcement was light on details. To learn more about EXPO ULL, I stopped by G.Skill's Computex booth, where the company demonstrated four new kits that offer EXPO ULL support.Memory latency directly affects how long the CPU has to wait in order to get data back from RAM, and so it has a major impact on CPU performance. But even as new DDR standards and ever-faster DIMMs have boosted memory bandwidth, DDR latency has improved at a much slower pace over time.For some very high-level background, when selecting memory, PC builders will generally consider a given memory kit's speed and its CAS latency (CL). If you compare two CL30 memory kits, for example, the one with the higher clock rate will have a lower effective latency in nanoseconds (because CL30 expresses a number of clock cycles).Latest Videos FromWatch full video here: Knowing this, your first instinct for reducing latency might be to seek the highest-clocked memory you can find with the lowest CAS latency (like DDR5-8400 or even faster modules).But on modern AMD platforms, it's not that simple. Reaching memory speeds higher than 6000 MT/s generally requires the use of a 1:2 multiplier mode between the clock of the integrated memory controller (the UCLK), which generally tops out around 3000 MHz, and the memory clock (MCLK). This 1:2 multiplier adds latency, and so it can counterintuitively reduce performance even as memory speeds climb above 6000 MT/s. (Remember that DDR memory moves bits at twice the clock rate, hence MT/s). You may like AMD says new EXPO ‘Ultra Low Latency’ DDR5 memory should be 'effectively the same price' as current kits AMD promises 13% uplift with new EXPO ‘Ultra Low Latency’ overclocking on DDR5 DIMMs AMD's memory-boosting EXPO 1.2 is here, adds support for three Chinese memory vendors With this 1:2 multiplier active, by the time additional memory clock speed even begins to bring latency back down to where it would generally be in the 1:1 mode, you're looking at wildly expensive and exotic memory kits, and so most enthusiasts running Ryzen 7000 and Ryzen 9000 CPUs consider it desirable to choose memory that lets them run the UCLK and MCLK in 1:1 lockstep for the best balance of low latency and (relatively) low cost.All that is why using memory faster than 6000 MT/s on AMD platforms is generally counterproductive for gaming performance. That's why modules in the range of DDR5-6000 CL30 are widely regarded as the overclocking "sweet spot" for Ryzen 7000 and Ryzen 9000 CPUs.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsorsBut that doesn't mean there isn't further room for improvement, as the introduction of EXPO ULL suggests.G.Skill told me that until now, DRAM module makers were only permitted to change the four primary timings within EXPO (and XMP) profiles, leaving performance on the table. EXPO ULL affords memory makers more freedom to adjust the sub-timings within each of those four primary timings for even lower latencies, and to include those results in the memory's SPD. (Image credit: Future)Tweaking memory sub-timings on Ryzen platforms using community-made tools to determine the best potential settings used to be a fairly common practice for those seeking the best performance from their AMD systems, but Ryzen X3D processors and their massive slices of 3D V-Cache reduced those CPUs' sensitivity to those finer adjustments. It's become much more common to just get a DDR5-6000 CL30 kit, enable EXPO, and call it good. What to read next EXPO 1.2 will come to 600-series AM5 motherboards later, tech only enables partial CUDIMM support New 'HUDIMM' test shows nearly 50% reduction in memory throughput with single subchannel DDR5 Ambitious hacker reduces worst-case memory latency by up to 93%, but with severe downsides But if you are focused on achieving the absolute lowest memory latency, EXPO ULL removes the need to perform the (tedious and tricky) process of determining those improved sub-timings by allowing memory makers to shoulder that work and include it as part of the one-click boost that EXPO provides.For all that, EXPO ULL doesn't change the fundamental performance characteristics of X3D versus non-X3D CPUs, so while you can certainly pair an X3D chip with an EXPO ULL kit, G.Skill tells me that you're not going to see as large of a difference in performance from that pairing as you would with a non-X3D chip. That's why AMD is touting the performance gains of EXPO ULL with a Ryzen 7 9700X and not the Ryzen 7 9850X3D you might expect.G.Skill also told me that EXPO ULL-ready memory requires stricter binning of individual memory chips during production, so it isn't just a software change that can be applied to existing modules. The company says the extra work involved in this stricter binning process means that modules supporting the feature are likely to be more expensive than kits that haven't undergone the same characterization.Overall, then, EXPO ULL is likely to be a premium (and somewhat niche) addition to the EXPO program instead of a broad replacement for non-ULL EXPO profiles. Demanding gamers who need the lowest memory latency for the best performance in CPU-bound gaming scenarios will likely want an EXPO ULL kit regardless of the type of Ryzen CPU they're using. But we'll have to see just how much extra cash these kits demand in today's already eye-watering memory market and what benefit they have, if any, for AMD's massively popular X3D chips.
items.author: Jeffrey Kampman
items.titleZh: G.Skill解释AMD EXPO ULL如何解锁额外性能——扩展配置文件首次允许内存制造商包含子时序调整
items.descriptionZh:
AMD在2026年台北电脑展上发布了EXPO Ultra Low Latency（超低延迟）内存超频方案，旨在为用户提供一键式降低内存延迟的便捷途径。然而，最初的公告信息较为简略。为了深入了解EXPO ULL的细节，笔者造访了G.Skill的展台，该公司展示了四款支持EXPO ULL的新内存套件。

内存延迟直接影响CPU从内存读取数据的等待时间，因此对CPU性能影响巨大。尽管新的DDR标准和更快的DIMM模块提升了内存带宽，但延迟的改善速度远慢于带宽提升。在选择内存时，玩家通常关注内存频率和CAS延迟（CL）。例如，两款同为CL30的内存套件，频率更高的那款在纳秒级别的有效延迟更低（因为CL30表示时钟周期数）。直觉上，降低延迟似乎应追求最高频率和最低CAS延迟（如DDR5-8400甚至更快）。但在现代AMD平台上，情况并非如此简单。

在AMD平台（如Ryzen 7000/9000系列）上，要达成超过6000 MT/s的内存频率，通常需要启用内存控制器时钟（UCLK）与内存时钟（MCLK）之间的1:2倍频模式（UCLK最高约3000 MHz）。这种1:2倍频模式会引入额外延迟，导致即使内存频率超过6000 MT/s，性能反而可能下降。而如果使用1:1锁步模式（UCLK=MCLK），则能获得最低的延迟和相对较低的成本。因此，DDR5-6000 CL30被广泛认为是Ryzen 7000/9000系列的超频“甜点区间”。EXPO ULL的出现则暗示着仍有进一步优化的空间。

G.Skill透露，此前DRAM模块制造商只能在EXPO（及Intel XMP）配置文件中调整四个主要时序（primary timings），这限制了性能潜力。EXPO ULL允许内存制造商在这四个主要时序内进一步调整子时序（sub-timings），以实现更低的延迟，并将这些优化结果写入内存SPD芯片。过去，一些追求极致性能的玩家会手动调整子时序，但Ryzen X3D处理器（带有3D V-Cache）降低了对这些精细调整的敏感度，因此玩家更倾向于直接购买DDR5-6000 CL30套件并一键开启EXPO。现在，EXPO ULL消除了手动调试的繁琐和风险，由制造商完成优化，用户只需一键即可获得更低延迟。

然而，EXPO ULL并未改变X3D与非X3D CPU的基本性能特征。G.Skill表示，使用X3D芯片搭配EXPO ULL内存，性能提升不如非X3D芯片明显。因此，AMD宣传EXPO ULL性能增益时使用了Ryzen 7 9700X而非Ryzen 7 9850X3D。此外，EXPO ULL就绪内存需要更严格的芯片分档（binning），因此不仅是软件层面的改动，无法应用于现有模块。更严格的筛选和额外工作意味着支持该特性的内存套件可能比普通套件更贵。

总体而言，EXPO ULL很可能是EXPO计划中的一项高端（且有些小众）补充，而非广泛替代现有EXPO配置。对于追求最低内存延迟以获得最佳CPU绑定游戏性能的玩家来说，无论使用何种Ryzen CPU，EXPO ULL套件都值得关注。但最终仍需看这些套件在当前价格高昂的内存市场中要价多少，以及它们对于大受欢迎的X3D芯片究竟能带来多少实际收益。

items.title: Here comes new Siri again
items.description:
Our first glimpse of the new AI Siri came all the way back at WWDC 2024.	

Apple has been on its back foot, AI-wise, for the past few years. But in a strange way, playing from behind might not be such a bad move.
At WWDC on Monday, Apple appears to be getting ready to reintroduce us to the new Siri. Again. As a reminder, we met the new Siri in 2024 when Apple "launched" Apple Intelligence. Siri came with a new glowing border, different voice options, and the ability to punt questions to ChatGPT. The whole "Intelligence" bit of the Siri redesign was coming soon, Apple promised. It didn't. In fact, its promotion around Apple Intelligence was so misleading that the company is settling a class-action lawsuit and has t …
Read the full story at The Verge.
items.content: Apple has been on its back foot, AI-wise, for the past few years. But in a strange way, playing from behind might not be such a bad move.At WWDC on Monday, Apple appears to be getting ready to reintroduce us to the new Siri. Again. As a reminder, we met the new Siri in 2024 when Apple “launched” Apple Intelligence. Siri came with a new glowing border, different voice options, and the ability to punt questions to ChatGPT. The whole “Intelligence” bit of the Siri redesign was coming soon, Apple promised. It didn’t. In fact, its promotion around Apple Intelligence was so misleading that the company is settling a class-action lawsuit and has to pay iPhone owners for the features it never shipped. The funny thing is, by fumbling the ball so badly, Apple might have just fallen backward into an advantageous position.Let’s be clear; if such a thing as a race to an AI assistant exists, Apple is losing badly. Gemini is already doing things like ordering Ubers and DoorDashing teriyaki. It can look at your calendar and figure out when you should leave for the airport. Gemini won the race, fair and square.Gemini is already doing things like ordering Ubers and DoorDashing teriyakiBut there’s also a growing distrust of AI, particularly from young people, and the better Gemini gets, the creepier it is. It has to be if it’s going to deliver on the promise of a truly helpful assistant. But wanting your AI assistant to anticipate your next move and actually watching it happen? Those are very different things. I willingly gave Gemini permission to access my Google Photos and Gmail, but it always makes my skin crawl hearing Gemini say my son’s name out loud. I test out a lot of this stuff as it becomes available — hazard of the job — but the public reaction when these kinds of features start trickling down to the mainstream will be very telling.New New Siri will be built on top of Gemini in some fashion. Apple is no doubt paying handsomely for the privilege, but there’s a potential upside to being one step removed in this way. You know what company doesn’t have its name attached to a big, unpopular data center project? Apple. Google isn’t winning friends and influencing people by rushing to start massive construction projects in backyards across the country. Apple gets to keep its hands clean, even if its payments to Google are presumably being funneled toward the great data center buildout.RelatedThe more young people use AI, the more they hate itThen there’s the Copilot of it all; the AI-buttons-everywhere factor. Siri’s attempts to summarize messages are amusing and often annoying, but at least Siri isn’t all up in every one of my work documents begging to summarize it for me. On the other hand, you can’t open a Google app without coming face-to-face with a Gemini sparkle these days, and it risks getting real old, real fast.Don’t get me wrong; I think Apple would love to put Siri to work writing my emails, perfecting my photos into “memories,” and talking me through the next steps to rehabilitate the dying plants in my yard. It’s just that Siri can’t really do any of that yet. When we meet this new Gemini-enhanced Siri, it’ll be telling to see where and how aggressively it surfaces. According to Bloomberg’s reporting, it sure sounds like we’re going to see it in a lot of places: the Dynamic Island, Photos, maybe even its own dedicated Siri app for the first time. That’s a very different Siri from the timer-setting voice assistant we currently know, mostly hiding behind the scenes.Remember this tagline from the first time Apple tried to launch AI Siri? Two years ago? Yeah, me neither.I suspect Apple is also going to play up the thing it already loves playing up: privacy. You can bet we’ll hear more about Private Cloud Compute, which supposedly keeps your data as secure as if it had never left your device. The updated Siri may also come with the option to automatically delete chats after a certain period of time, rather than holding onto that data by default. Promising a more private, secure AI experience might appeal to people who are squeamish about handing over even more personal info to Google. But it doesn’t do much for someone who’s just sick of having AI in their face all day in every piece of software.An advantage, especially the kind you stumble on, can disappear as quickly as it arrived.Apple could easily cast its slow AI rollout as the more responsible move. Google execs used to constantly talk about being “bold and responsible” with AI, but lately they’re too busy firing off new Gemini features and basking in the foothills of the singularity to dwell on that much. Passing off the delays as taking the time to do things right isn’t a bad bet, but the time for false starts is over. Siri’s going to have to pull it off for real this time; when a second chance like this comes around, you can’t count on it coming back.
items.author: Allison Johnson
items.descriptionZh:
过去几年，苹果在人工智能领域一直处于落后态势。但奇怪的是，这种“从后方追赶”的局面或许并非坏事。在即将召开的WWDC上，苹果似乎准备再次向我们介绍“新版Siri”。回想起来，我们在2024年苹果“发布”Apple Intelligence时已经见过一次新版Siri——它带着发光边框、不同的语音选项，以及将问题转给ChatGPT的能力。苹果当时承诺，“Intelligence”部分的Siri重设计“即将到来”，但最终并未实现。事实上，围绕Apple Intelligence的宣传极具误导性，以至于公司正在和解一起集体诉讼，需向iPhone用户赔偿那些从未推出的功能。讽刺的是，正是这次严重的失误，可能让苹果意外跌入了一个有利位置。

明确说，如果存在“AI助手竞赛”，苹果正在惨败。Gemini已经能帮你叫Uber、点外卖、查看日历并规划出发去机场的时间——它公平地赢得了比赛。但与此同时，公众对AI的信任正在下降，尤其是年轻人。Gemini越强大，越让人感到不安：为了成为真正有用的助手，它必须足够“侵入”，但渴望AI预判你的下一步行动和亲眼看着它执行是两回事。我自愿授予Gemini访问我的Google相册和Gmail的权限，但听到它大声说出我儿子的名字时，总会头皮发麻。我作为从业者测试这些功能是职责所在，但主流用户的真实反应将很有说服力。

新版Siri将以某种方式建立在Gemini之上。苹果无疑为此支付了高昂费用，但作为“第二层”角色可能带来好处：哪个公司没有因为大型数据中心项目而背上恶名？苹果没有。谷歌正因在全美各地匆忙启动大型建设而失去朋友和影响力。苹果得以保持清白形象，即便它支付给谷歌的钱很可能被用于数据中心建设。此外，还有微软Copilot那种“AI按钮无处不在”的问题：Siri尝试总结消息时虽然有趣又恼人，但至少不会在每个工作文档里乞求总结；而如今打开任何Google应用都逃不开Gemini的闪烁，这种体验很快就会让人厌倦。

别误会，苹果当然希望让Siri帮你写邮件、优化照片生成“回忆”、指导你拯救院里的枯萎植物——只是Siri目前什么都做不到。当我们见到这个由Gemini增强的新Siri时，关键将看它在哪些位置以多激进的姿态出现。据彭博社报道，我们很可能在灵动岛、照片应用，甚至首次出现的独立Siri应用中看到它。这与目前那个只负责设置定时器、躲在后台的语音助手截然不同。我怀疑苹果还会大力宣扬它最擅长的隐私牌：私有云计算（Private Cloud Compute）将确保数据像从未离开过设备一样安全；新版Siri可能提供自动定期删除聊天记录的选项，而非默认保留数据。承诺更私密、更安全的AI体验，或许能吸引那些对向谷歌交出更多个人信息感到不安的用户。但这对于整天被AI侵扰、已经厌倦的人来说，作用有限。

优势，尤其是偶然获得的优势，可能迅速消失。苹果很容易将缓慢的AI推出包装成更负责任的做法。谷歌高管过去常谈论“既大胆又负责任”，但如今忙于推出新功能、沉浸在奇点临近的兴奋中，无暇顾及。将延迟解释为“花时间把事情做好”并非坏招，但假启动的机会已经耗尽。Siri这次必须真正成功——当这样的第二次机会来临时，不能指望它还会再来。

history.title: AMD B650 expansion cards hit retail starting at $199 — add four M.2 PCIe 4.0 slots and 11 USB ports to any PC with a PCIe slot
history.description: A couple of new add-in cards exemplify the trend of slapping AMD's Promontory 21 chipset onto a card for extra I/O expansion.
history.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 1 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter It's a weird fact of the modern PC industry, but the motherboard "chipset" or "Platform Controller Hub" (to use Intel's terminology) is basically just a PCIe-attached I/O hub that exposes additional PCIe, USB, SATA, and platform services. Contemporary CPUs are built as SoCs, so all the essential functions to run the machine are integrated into the CPU itself. That's why companies have started creating what are effectively I/O breakout boards by slapping motherboard chipsets onto PCI Express cards, including the one pictured above from WisdPi, as well as one coming from Minisforum, as spotted by PC Watch at Computex.In the case of the AMD Promontory 21 chipset, it really is just a PCI Express I/O controller. In fact, AMD created the X670 chipset by simply wiring up two Promontory 21 chips in series. It's the exact same chip, just used in pairs on the fancier motherboards, so you end up with piles of I/O, though it's all running over the same PCI Express 4.0 x4 link to the CPU. Two of the M.2 slots are on the front of the card, while a large black heatsink covers the Promontory 21 chip. (Image credit: WisdPi)We've reported on cards like this before; this past December, we highlighted an open-source project for exactly this purpose, and of course, Asusd and ASRock have shipped expansion boards for specific motherboards that "upgrade" them from B650 to X670 by adding the second Promontory 21 chipset. All the way back in 2023, we also covered AMD engineers showing off the same thing in a Gamers Nexus video. Indeed, the very word "Promontory" refers to a projection from a larger body, such as a high cliff or, perhaps, an add-on card.Latest Videos FromWatch full video here: So, the WisdPi "PROM21 All In Expansion Card" is a half-height PCI Express 4.0 add-in card that offers a whopping four M.2 slots, five USB 10 Gbps ports, six USB 2.0 ports, and an OCuLink port that supports PCI Express 4.0 or can breakout into four SATA ports. That's more connectivity than some motherboards offer, which is no surprise, since the card costs $199 by itself, with no cables. WisdPi is primarily a Raspberry Pi shop, and as you'd expect, the card is compatible with Raspberry Pi devices that expose PCI Express, but that's simply because it's actually compatible with any PCI Express device, from AMD, Intel, Arm, or whoever. The other two M.2 slots are on the back of the card, which could complicate cooling. (Image credit: WisdPi)The card spotted by PC Watch at Minisforum's Computex booth is similar in that it also offers four M.2 slots, an OCuLink connector, and at least one high-speed 20 Gbps USB port. However, it lacks the SATA functionality and the other USB ports of the WisdPi offering. Also, unlike WisdPi's card, it seems that it will come with a dedicated cooling unit (with a blower fan and a shroud) for fast SSDs. That's a little ironic considering the card seemingly limits drives to PCIe 4.0 x2 if you install all four, but "cooling" is better than "no cooling". No word on how much the Minisforum offering will cost, but it will apparently be available in Q3 of this year. You may like This platypus PCIe adapter converts half-height GPUs into full-height while adding two M.2 slots for SSDs New flat M.2 SSD adapter doesn't stick out from the motherboard, unlocking PCIe slots hidden under massive GPUs ASRock launches new Frankensteined motherboard with one DDR4 slot and two DDR5 slots It's almost a shame these cards aren't more commonplace. For folks with machines based on entry-level motherboards, one of these cards could be just the ticket to expanding the I/O. Particularly on older-generation motherboards that usually had only one M.2 slot (if any), a card like one of these could enable significant storage expansion now that M.2 SSDs are the only type still being actively produced. You can check out PC Watch's photos of the Minisforum "PCIE TO 4" card at their post over here.Follow Tom's Hardware on Google News, or add us as a preferred source, to get our latest news, analysis, & reviews in your feeds.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsors
history.author: Zak Killian
history.titleZh: AMD B650扩展卡上市零售价199美元起——为任何拥有PCIe插槽的PC添加四个M.2 PCIe 4.0插槽和11个USB端口
history.descriptionZh:
现代PC行业存在一个奇特现象：主板“芯片组”或英特尔所谓的“平台控制器中枢”（Platform Controller Hub），本质上只是一个通过PCIe连接的I/O集线器，用于提供额外的PCIe、USB、SATA及平台服务。如今的CPU已集成SoC设计，运行机器所需的核心功能都包含在CPU内部。因此，一些公司开始将主板芯片组直接封装到PCI Express扩展卡上，做成实际的I/O扩展板——例如WisdPi推出的PROM21 All In扩展卡，以及Computex展会上Minisforum展示的类似产品（PC Watch报道）。

### 核心技术原理与创新点

以AMD Promontory 21芯片组为例，它本质上就是一个PCI Express I/O控制器。有趣的是，AMD在高端X670主板上通过串联两颗Promontory 21芯片来实现更多I/O接口——其实就是同一颗芯片，在高端主板上成对使用，从而获得大量I/O扩展能力，但所有这些接口都通过同一条PCI Express 4.0 x4链路连接到CPU。WisdPi的“PROM21 All In Expansion Card”正是基于这一设计，将Promontory 21芯片作为半高PCIe 4.0扩展卡推出。卡正面覆盖大型黑色散热片，两个M.2插槽设于正面，另两个M.2插槽位于背面（可能影响散热），此外还有五个USB 10Gbps端口、六个USB 2.0端口和一个支持PCIe 4.0的OCuLink端口（可扩展为四个SATA端口）。该卡本身售价199美元（不含线缆）。

此前类似产品已有报道：2023年12月我们报道过一个开源项目，华硕和华擎也推出过针对特定主板的扩展板，可从B650“升级”到X670（即添加第二颗Promontory 21芯片）。早在2023年，AMD工程师就在Gamers Nexus视频中展示过相同概念。有趣的是，“Promontory”一词本意是“从较大山体突出的部分（如悬崖）”，恰好对应这种附加卡的角色。

### 性能参数与对比

WisdPi卡提供四个M.2插槽（PCIe 4.0）、五个10Gbps USB口、六个USB 2.0、一个OCuLink（可转4个SATA）。这一连接能力甚至超过某些主板的原生接口，但注意所有I/O共享一条PCIe 4.0 x4链路（约8GB/s带宽）。该卡兼容任何PCIe设备（AMD、Intel、Arm平台），包括树莓派等单板计算机——只要它们有PCIe接口。

Minisforum在Computex上展示的类似卡（暂名“PCIE TO 4”）同样提供四个M.2插槽、一个OCuLink接口和至少一个20Gbps高速USB端口，但缺少SATA功能和其他USB端口。值得注意的是，Minisforum版似乎会配备专用散热单元（含吹风风扇和导流罩），适合高速SSD——这有点讽刺，因为如果全部安装四个M.2 SSD，每个仅能获得PCIe 4.0 x2带宽，但“有散热”总比“没散热”好。Minisforum卡预计2024年Q3上市，价格未公布。

### 技术影响与应用场景

这类扩展卡尤其适合使用入门级主板（通常只有一两个M.2插槽）的用户，特别是老一代主板往往只有一个甚至没有M.2插槽。在现代M.2 SSD是唯一仍在积极生产的存储类型的背景下，这类卡可大幅扩展存储能力。对于树莓派等小型PCIe设备，它们也能瞬间获得丰富的I/O扩展。可惜这些卡尚未普及——如果能更常见，将极大提升旧平台和小型系统的I/O灵活性。感兴趣的读者可以查看PC Watch关于Minisforum卡的详细照片报道。

history.title: Upgrade your PC with a 2TB PCIe 4.0 SSD, 750W power supply, and 240mm AIO for $300 — hardware bundle saves you nearly $200
history.description: Even though power supplies and CPU coolers have remained unaffected by the AI boom, storage costs have skyrocketed, which makes this deal even more lucrative. You're getting a blazing-fast 2TB SSD, a genuinely solid power supply, and a decent AIO that can help you stop worrying about the small things and focus on sourcing cheaper memory and GPUs.
history.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 0 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter Despite the AI boom driving up prices and making it harder to build a PC today, there are still some good deals floating around. We've just found one from MSI, and it's a bundle that includes a 2TB PCIe 4.0 SSD, a 240mm AIO liquid cooler, and a 750W 80+ Gold power supply with native ATX 3.1 compatibility. It's called the "SPATIUM M470 PRO 2TB SSD Refresh Kit," and it's available for just $299 right now, down from $492. Check out this deal on the MSI Store As the name suggests, the bundle includes an MSI Spatium M470 Pro SSD in a 2TB capacity. We've reviewed the non-Pro variant of this drive and found it adequate. This "Pro" model is actually DRAM-less but still features sequential read speeds up to 6,000 MB/s and write speeds up to 5,000 MB/s, with up to 640 TBW of endurance. The Phison E27T controller powers it and comes with a 5-year warranty.Next up, there's a MAG CoreLiquid A13 240mm AIO part of the kit, too. It's a pretty run-of-the-mill liquid cooler that supports recent CPU families. It has ARGB fans and subtle lighting on the pump block, with a pretty minimal overall design. The pump speed can be modulated between 300 RPM and 3,800 RPM, with the fans reaching up to 200 RPM, pushing up to 62.6 CFM of airflow. We found the CoreLiquid A13 to be a decent option in our review — nothing special, but it can definitely get the job done. Save 39% ($192.01)MSI Spatium M470 Pro 2TB SSD Refresh Kit: was $492 now $299.99 at AmazonThis bundle includes a Spatium M470 Pro 2TB SSD with PCIe 4.0 x4 connectivity for blazing fast speeds. You also get a MAG CoreLiquid A13 240mm AIO with ARGB fans and pump. Lastly, the MAG A750GLS power supply rounds out the kit with its 80+ Gold rating and native 16-pin connectors.View Deal Lastly, you'll also get the MAG A750GLS power supply in the bundle; it's a 750W PCIe 5.0 unit with an 80+ Gold rating. That means it comes with a native 16-pin connector to power the hungriest GPUs, and the cable has a yellow end that can help identify loose connections. A 10-year warranty backs the A750GLS and comes with enough sleeved cables to cover all your needs. It's also a well-reviewed model in the community.All in all, you're building a solid base with these components that can help make the hunt for more important parts, such as the GPU and memory, easier. For just $299 on the MSI Store right now, it doesn't get much better than this. Even if you were to go for a cheaper cooler and SSD on its own and ditch the bundle, it would cost you around $400 to source the parts individually, so don't miss out on this deal! If you're looking for more savings, check out our Best PC Hardware deals for a range of products, or dive deeper into our specialized SSD and Storage Deals, Hard Drive Deals, Gaming Monitor Deals, Graphics Card Deals, gaming chair, or CPU Deals pages.
history.author: Hassam Nasir
history.titleZh: 用2TB PCIe 4.0固态硬盘、750W电源和240mm一体式水冷升级你的电脑，仅需300美元——硬件套装省近200美元
history.descriptionZh:
在AI浪潮推动下，PC硬件价格普遍上涨、组装难度增加，但市场上仍存在一些高性价比的选择。微星（MSI）近期推出了一款名为“SPATIUM M470 PRO 2TB SSD Refresh Kit”的套装，包含一块2TB PCIe 4.0固态硬盘、一个240mm一体式水冷散热器，以及一个750W 80+ Gold认证、原生ATX 3.1兼容的电源，目前售价仅为299美元（原价492美元），降幅达39%。该套装在MSI官方商店有售。

套装核心之一是微星Spatium M470 Pro固态硬盘，容量2TB。这款“Pro”型号采用无DRAM缓存设计，但依然能提供高达6000 MB/s的顺序读取速度和5000 MB/s的顺序写入速度，耐久度达到640 TBW。它由群联Phison E27T主控驱动，并享有5年质保。虽然我们此前评测过非Pro版本且认为表现尚可，但Pro版本在无DRAM方案中属于性能较好的选择。

散热部分为MAG CoreLiquid A13 240mm一体式水冷，属于中规中矩的常规产品，支持主流CPU平台。它配备ARGB风扇，泵头带有柔和灯光，整体设计简约。水泵转速可在300-3800 RPM之间调节，风扇最高转速2000 RPM（原文可能笔误写为200 RPM？实际应为2000 RPM左右），最大风量62.6 CFM。在我们的评测中，该水冷表现不错，虽不惊艳但足以胜任日常散热需求。

电源是MAG A750GLS，额定功率750W，符合PCIe 5.0标准，获得80+ Gold金牌认证。它配备原生16针（12VHPWR）接口，可以为高端显卡供电，并且该线缆末端采用黄色设计以帮助用户识别是否插紧。电源提供10年质保，附带足够多的编制线缆，满足各类装机需求，在社区中口碑良好。

总体而言，这套装机核心部件为组装一台高性能PC奠定了扎实基础，后续只需再购买显卡和内存即可。如果单独购买类似规格的散热器、SSD和电源，总价大约需要400美元，而套装仅299美元，性价比非常突出。该套装适合正在攒机、追求高速存储和稳定供电的用户，尤其适合升级老平台或搭建中高配游戏主机。如需更多优惠信息，可关注PC硬件特价、SSD存储、硬盘、游戏显示器、显卡、游戏椅及CPU等专项折扣页面。

history.title: G.Skill explains how AMD EXPO ULL unlocks additional performance — expanded profiles allow memory makers to include subtiming tweaks for the first time
history.description: AMD's EXPO Ultra Low Latency program, announced at Computex 2026, aims to give users a one-click route to lower memory latencies than its existing EXPO profiles
history.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 1 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter AMD's EXPO Ultra Low Latency program, announced at Computex 2026, aims to give users a one-click route to lower memory latencies than its existing EXPO profiles, but the company's initial announcement was light on details. To learn more about EXPO ULL, I stopped by G.Skill's Computex booth, where the company demonstrated four new kits that offer EXPO ULL support.Memory latency directly affects how long the CPU has to wait in order to get data back from RAM, and so it has a major impact on CPU performance. But even as new DDR standards and ever-faster DIMMs have boosted memory bandwidth, DDR latency has improved at a much slower pace over time.For some very high-level background, when selecting memory, PC builders will generally consider a given memory kit's speed and its CAS latency (CL). If you compare two CL30 memory kits, for example, the one with the higher clock rate will have a lower effective latency in nanoseconds (because CL30 expresses a number of clock cycles).Latest Videos FromWatch full video here: Knowing this, your first instinct for reducing latency might be to seek the highest-clocked memory you can find with the lowest CAS latency (like DDR5-8400 or even faster modules).But on modern AMD platforms, it's not that simple. Reaching memory speeds higher than 6000 MT/s generally requires the use of a 1:2 multiplier mode between the clock of the integrated memory controller (the UCLK), which generally tops out around 3000 MHz, and the memory clock (MCLK). This 1:2 multiplier adds latency, and so it can counterintuitively reduce performance even as memory speeds climb above 6000 MT/s. (Remember that DDR memory moves bits at twice the clock rate, hence MT/s). You may like AMD says new EXPO ‘Ultra Low Latency’ DDR5 memory should be 'effectively the same price' as current kits AMD promises 13% uplift with new EXPO ‘Ultra Low Latency’ overclocking on DDR5 DIMMs AMD's memory-boosting EXPO 1.2 is here, adds support for three Chinese memory vendors With this 1:2 multiplier active, by the time additional memory clock speed even begins to bring latency back down to where it would generally be in the 1:1 mode, you're looking at wildly expensive and exotic memory kits, and so most enthusiasts running Ryzen 7000 and Ryzen 9000 CPUs consider it desirable to choose memory that lets them run the UCLK and MCLK in 1:1 lockstep for the best balance of low latency and (relatively) low cost.All that is why using memory faster than 6000 MT/s on AMD platforms is generally counterproductive for gaming performance. That's why modules in the range of DDR5-6000 CL30 are widely regarded as the overclocking "sweet spot" for Ryzen 7000 and Ryzen 9000 CPUs.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsorsBut that doesn't mean there isn't further room for improvement, as the introduction of EXPO ULL suggests.G.Skill told me that until now, DRAM module makers were only permitted to change the four primary timings within EXPO (and XMP) profiles, leaving performance on the table. EXPO ULL affords memory makers more freedom to adjust the sub-timings within each of those four primary timings for even lower latencies, and to include those results in the memory's SPD. (Image credit: Future)Tweaking memory sub-timings on Ryzen platforms using community-made tools to determine the best potential settings used to be a fairly common practice for those seeking the best performance from their AMD systems, but Ryzen X3D processors and their massive slices of 3D V-Cache reduced those CPUs' sensitivity to those finer adjustments. It's become much more common to just get a DDR5-6000 CL30 kit, enable EXPO, and call it good. What to read next EXPO 1.2 will come to 600-series AM5 motherboards later, tech only enables partial CUDIMM support New 'HUDIMM' test shows nearly 50% reduction in memory throughput with single subchannel DDR5 Ambitious hacker reduces worst-case memory latency by up to 93%, but with severe downsides But if you are focused on achieving the absolute lowest memory latency, EXPO ULL removes the need to perform the (tedious and tricky) process of determining those improved sub-timings by allowing memory makers to shoulder that work and include it as part of the one-click boost that EXPO provides.For all that, EXPO ULL doesn't change the fundamental performance characteristics of X3D versus non-X3D CPUs, so while you can certainly pair an X3D chip with an EXPO ULL kit, G.Skill tells me that you're not going to see as large of a difference in performance from that pairing as you would with a non-X3D chip. That's why AMD is touting the performance gains of EXPO ULL with a Ryzen 7 9700X and not the Ryzen 7 9850X3D you might expect.G.Skill also told me that EXPO ULL-ready memory requires stricter binning of individual memory chips during production, so it isn't just a software change that can be applied to existing modules. The company says the extra work involved in this stricter binning process means that modules supporting the feature are likely to be more expensive than kits that haven't undergone the same characterization.Overall, then, EXPO ULL is likely to be a premium (and somewhat niche) addition to the EXPO program instead of a broad replacement for non-ULL EXPO profiles. Demanding gamers who need the lowest memory latency for the best performance in CPU-bound gaming scenarios will likely want an EXPO ULL kit regardless of the type of Ryzen CPU they're using. But we'll have to see just how much extra cash these kits demand in today's already eye-watering memory market and what benefit they have, if any, for AMD's massively popular X3D chips.
history.author: Jeffrey Kampman
history.titleZh: G.Skill解释AMD EXPO ULL如何解锁额外性能——扩展配置文件首次允许内存制造商包含子时序调整
history.descriptionZh:
AMD在2026年台北电脑展上发布了EXPO Ultra Low Latency（超低延迟）内存超频方案，旨在为用户提供一键式降低内存延迟的便捷途径。然而，最初的公告信息较为简略。为了深入了解EXPO ULL的细节，笔者造访了G.Skill的展台，该公司展示了四款支持EXPO ULL的新内存套件。

内存延迟直接影响CPU从内存读取数据的等待时间，因此对CPU性能影响巨大。尽管新的DDR标准和更快的DIMM模块提升了内存带宽，但延迟的改善速度远慢于带宽提升。在选择内存时，玩家通常关注内存频率和CAS延迟（CL）。例如，两款同为CL30的内存套件，频率更高的那款在纳秒级别的有效延迟更低（因为CL30表示时钟周期数）。直觉上，降低延迟似乎应追求最高频率和最低CAS延迟（如DDR5-8400甚至更快）。但在现代AMD平台上，情况并非如此简单。

在AMD平台（如Ryzen 7000/9000系列）上，要达成超过6000 MT/s的内存频率，通常需要启用内存控制器时钟（UCLK）与内存时钟（MCLK）之间的1:2倍频模式（UCLK最高约3000 MHz）。这种1:2倍频模式会引入额外延迟，导致即使内存频率超过6000 MT/s，性能反而可能下降。而如果使用1:1锁步模式（UCLK=MCLK），则能获得最低的延迟和相对较低的成本。因此，DDR5-6000 CL30被广泛认为是Ryzen 7000/9000系列的超频“甜点区间”。EXPO ULL的出现则暗示着仍有进一步优化的空间。

G.Skill透露，此前DRAM模块制造商只能在EXPO（及Intel XMP）配置文件中调整四个主要时序（primary timings），这限制了性能潜力。EXPO ULL允许内存制造商在这四个主要时序内进一步调整子时序（sub-timings），以实现更低的延迟，并将这些优化结果写入内存SPD芯片。过去，一些追求极致性能的玩家会手动调整子时序，但Ryzen X3D处理器（带有3D V-Cache）降低了对这些精细调整的敏感度，因此玩家更倾向于直接购买DDR5-6000 CL30套件并一键开启EXPO。现在，EXPO ULL消除了手动调试的繁琐和风险，由制造商完成优化，用户只需一键即可获得更低延迟。

然而，EXPO ULL并未改变X3D与非X3D CPU的基本性能特征。G.Skill表示，使用X3D芯片搭配EXPO ULL内存，性能提升不如非X3D芯片明显。因此，AMD宣传EXPO ULL性能增益时使用了Ryzen 7 9700X而非Ryzen 7 9850X3D。此外，EXPO ULL就绪内存需要更严格的芯片分档（binning），因此不仅是软件层面的改动，无法应用于现有模块。更严格的筛选和额外工作意味着支持该特性的内存套件可能比普通套件更贵。

总体而言，EXPO ULL很可能是EXPO计划中的一项高端（且有些小众）补充，而非广泛替代现有EXPO配置。对于追求最低内存延迟以获得最佳CPU绑定游戏性能的玩家来说，无论使用何种Ryzen CPU，EXPO ULL套件都值得关注。但最终仍需看这些套件在当前价格高昂的内存市场中要价多少，以及它们对于大受欢迎的X3D芯片究竟能带来多少实际收益。

history.title: Here comes new Siri again
history.description:
Our first glimpse of the new AI Siri came all the way back at WWDC 2024.	

Apple has been on its back foot, AI-wise, for the past few years. But in a strange way, playing from behind might not be such a bad move.
At WWDC on Monday, Apple appears to be getting ready to reintroduce us to the new Siri. Again. As a reminder, we met the new Siri in 2024 when Apple "launched" Apple Intelligence. Siri came with a new glowing border, different voice options, and the ability to punt questions to ChatGPT. The whole "Intelligence" bit of the Siri redesign was coming soon, Apple promised. It didn't. In fact, its promotion around Apple Intelligence was so misleading that the company is settling a class-action lawsuit and has t …
Read the full story at The Verge.
history.content: Apple has been on its back foot, AI-wise, for the past few years. But in a strange way, playing from behind might not be such a bad move.At WWDC on Monday, Apple appears to be getting ready to reintroduce us to the new Siri. Again. As a reminder, we met the new Siri in 2024 when Apple “launched” Apple Intelligence. Siri came with a new glowing border, different voice options, and the ability to punt questions to ChatGPT. The whole “Intelligence” bit of the Siri redesign was coming soon, Apple promised. It didn’t. In fact, its promotion around Apple Intelligence was so misleading that the company is settling a class-action lawsuit and has to pay iPhone owners for the features it never shipped. The funny thing is, by fumbling the ball so badly, Apple might have just fallen backward into an advantageous position.Let’s be clear; if such a thing as a race to an AI assistant exists, Apple is losing badly. Gemini is already doing things like ordering Ubers and DoorDashing teriyaki. It can look at your calendar and figure out when you should leave for the airport. Gemini won the race, fair and square.Gemini is already doing things like ordering Ubers and DoorDashing teriyakiBut there’s also a growing distrust of AI, particularly from young people, and the better Gemini gets, the creepier it is. It has to be if it’s going to deliver on the promise of a truly helpful assistant. But wanting your AI assistant to anticipate your next move and actually watching it happen? Those are very different things. I willingly gave Gemini permission to access my Google Photos and Gmail, but it always makes my skin crawl hearing Gemini say my son’s name out loud. I test out a lot of this stuff as it becomes available — hazard of the job — but the public reaction when these kinds of features start trickling down to the mainstream will be very telling.New New Siri will be built on top of Gemini in some fashion. Apple is no doubt paying handsomely for the privilege, but there’s a potential upside to being one step removed in this way. You know what company doesn’t have its name attached to a big, unpopular data center project? Apple. Google isn’t winning friends and influencing people by rushing to start massive construction projects in backyards across the country. Apple gets to keep its hands clean, even if its payments to Google are presumably being funneled toward the great data center buildout.RelatedThe more young people use AI, the more they hate itThen there’s the Copilot of it all; the AI-buttons-everywhere factor. Siri’s attempts to summarize messages are amusing and often annoying, but at least Siri isn’t all up in every one of my work documents begging to summarize it for me. On the other hand, you can’t open a Google app without coming face-to-face with a Gemini sparkle these days, and it risks getting real old, real fast.Don’t get me wrong; I think Apple would love to put Siri to work writing my emails, perfecting my photos into “memories,” and talking me through the next steps to rehabilitate the dying plants in my yard. It’s just that Siri can’t really do any of that yet. When we meet this new Gemini-enhanced Siri, it’ll be telling to see where and how aggressively it surfaces. According to Bloomberg’s reporting, it sure sounds like we’re going to see it in a lot of places: the Dynamic Island, Photos, maybe even its own dedicated Siri app for the first time. That’s a very different Siri from the timer-setting voice assistant we currently know, mostly hiding behind the scenes.Remember this tagline from the first time Apple tried to launch AI Siri? Two years ago? Yeah, me neither.I suspect Apple is also going to play up the thing it already loves playing up: privacy. You can bet we’ll hear more about Private Cloud Compute, which supposedly keeps your data as secure as if it had never left your device. The updated Siri may also come with the option to automatically delete chats after a certain period of time, rather than holding onto that data by default. Promising a more private, secure AI experience might appeal to people who are squeamish about handing over even more personal info to Google. But it doesn’t do much for someone who’s just sick of having AI in their face all day in every piece of software.An advantage, especially the kind you stumble on, can disappear as quickly as it arrived.Apple could easily cast its slow AI rollout as the more responsible move. Google execs used to constantly talk about being “bold and responsible” with AI, but lately they’re too busy firing off new Gemini features and basking in the foothills of the singularity to dwell on that much. Passing off the delays as taking the time to do things right isn’t a bad bet, but the time for false starts is over. Siri’s going to have to pull it off for real this time; when a second chance like this comes around, you can’t count on it coming back.
history.author: Allison Johnson
history.descriptionZh:
过去几年，苹果在人工智能领域一直处于落后态势。但奇怪的是，这种“从后方追赶”的局面或许并非坏事。在即将召开的WWDC上，苹果似乎准备再次向我们介绍“新版Siri”。回想起来，我们在2024年苹果“发布”Apple Intelligence时已经见过一次新版Siri——它带着发光边框、不同的语音选项，以及将问题转给ChatGPT的能力。苹果当时承诺，“Intelligence”部分的Siri重设计“即将到来”，但最终并未实现。事实上，围绕Apple Intelligence的宣传极具误导性，以至于公司正在和解一起集体诉讼，需向iPhone用户赔偿那些从未推出的功能。讽刺的是，正是这次严重的失误，可能让苹果意外跌入了一个有利位置。

明确说，如果存在“AI助手竞赛”，苹果正在惨败。Gemini已经能帮你叫Uber、点外卖、查看日历并规划出发去机场的时间——它公平地赢得了比赛。但与此同时，公众对AI的信任正在下降，尤其是年轻人。Gemini越强大，越让人感到不安：为了成为真正有用的助手，它必须足够“侵入”，但渴望AI预判你的下一步行动和亲眼看着它执行是两回事。我自愿授予Gemini访问我的Google相册和Gmail的权限，但听到它大声说出我儿子的名字时，总会头皮发麻。我作为从业者测试这些功能是职责所在，但主流用户的真实反应将很有说服力。

新版Siri将以某种方式建立在Gemini之上。苹果无疑为此支付了高昂费用，但作为“第二层”角色可能带来好处：哪个公司没有因为大型数据中心项目而背上恶名？苹果没有。谷歌正因在全美各地匆忙启动大型建设而失去朋友和影响力。苹果得以保持清白形象，即便它支付给谷歌的钱很可能被用于数据中心建设。此外，还有微软Copilot那种“AI按钮无处不在”的问题：Siri尝试总结消息时虽然有趣又恼人，但至少不会在每个工作文档里乞求总结；而如今打开任何Google应用都逃不开Gemini的闪烁，这种体验很快就会让人厌倦。

别误会，苹果当然希望让Siri帮你写邮件、优化照片生成“回忆”、指导你拯救院里的枯萎植物——只是Siri目前什么都做不到。当我们见到这个由Gemini增强的新Siri时，关键将看它在哪些位置以多激进的姿态出现。据彭博社报道，我们很可能在灵动岛、照片应用，甚至首次出现的独立Siri应用中看到它。这与目前那个只负责设置定时器、躲在后台的语音助手截然不同。我怀疑苹果还会大力宣扬它最擅长的隐私牌：私有云计算（Private Cloud Compute）将确保数据像从未离开过设备一样安全；新版Siri可能提供自动定期删除聊天记录的选项，而非默认保留数据。承诺更私密、更安全的AI体验，或许能吸引那些对向谷歌交出更多个人信息感到不安的用户。但这对于整天被AI侵扰、已经厌倦的人来说，作用有限。

优势，尤其是偶然获得的优势，可能迅速消失。苹果很容易将缓慢的AI推出包装成更负责任的做法。谷歌高管过去常谈论“既大胆又负责任”，但如今忙于推出新功能、沉浸在奇点临近的兴奋中，无暇顾及。将延迟解释为“花时间把事情做好”并非坏招，但假启动的机会已经耗尽。Siri这次必须真正成功——当这样的第二次机会来临时，不能指望它还会再来。
